steps.title: Identify the function of the examples
steps.content:
The passage makes a claim—”a new, more diverse generation of artists is stepping up to the podium”—and then provides evidence.

The two conductors are presented as specific instances that prove this claim.

The transition must signal the relationship between the claim and its supporting examples.

steps.title: Apply the correct transition signal
steps.content:
”For instance” introduces examples to support a preceding claim.

Alondra de la Parra and Lina Gonzalez-Granados are presented as specific instances demonstrating that the new generation is indeed stepping up.

The other choices fail to mark this explanatory role: “in addition” merely adds another item without signaling an exemplary relationship; “lastly” implies a final item in a longer sequence, but only two examples are given; “granted” signals concession rather than support.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
